title: 奉命谋杀
summary:
At the house where Tina is being treated, Corlston is instructed by a doctor to keep her warm and provide hot tea or coffee once she wakes. Mary Darland becomes agitated, shouting that Philip was fine and writing something just before she left him, questioning his stubbornness. Dr. Donald Craig disputes the assessment that Tina merely fainted, declaring that she was actually stabbed in the back and calling for an ambulance.

In a hotel room, Arthur Calgary reviews his notes and realizes he has focused too much on Mrs. Aggles. He shifts his focus to Jacqueline, evaluating various character assessments of her from Dr. McMaster, Leo Aigle, Hester, Kirsten Lindström, and Tina. He identifies a pattern involving money—specifically Mrs. Aigle’s trust fund, annuities, and bank accounts—as the "unknown factor" in the case. Calgary calls Hester, who informs him that Philip Darwent has been killed and Tina is in the hospital. Calgary promises to visit her after speaking with Chief Inspector Huishe.

Calgary meets with Inspector Huishe, who reports that Tina has woken up. Huishe explains that Philip Darwent was stabbed in the back while meddling in the murder case and notes that a knife with blood on it was found in Philip's pocket. Huishe suggests the killer is among them and that Tina may be withholding information due to fear, possibly related to her relationship with Mickey. Huishe provides a timeline: at 4:00, Mary Darland was in the kitchen while Leo Aigle, Gwendolyn Vaughan, Hester Aggell, and Kirsten Lindström were in other parts of the house; at 4:05, Mickey and Tina arrived, leading to Tina and Lindström discovering Philip's body.
